assertions.expectation: Clearly states that GKE auto-upgrades clusters automatically as the default and recommended model — this is what differentiates GKE from self-managed Kubernetes
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'GKE handles upgrades automatically' and 'this is actually the recommended approach for production environments' with detailed explanation of automatic control plane and node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows to control WHEN auto-upgrades happen (e.g., weekends, off-peak hours)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands for setting maintenance windows with examples of Sunday 2-6 AM timeframes and includes maintenance window configuration as a best practice

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance exclusions to control WHAT auto-upgrades happen (e.g., 'no minor or node upgrades' to block disruptive changes while allowing security patches)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions at all - it discusses disabling auto-upgrades entirely but not the concept of selectively excluding certain types of upgrades while allowing others

assertions.expectation: Does NOT recommend disabling auto-upgrades or switching to a purely manual upgrade cadence as the first option
assertions.reason: Response recommends keeping auto-upgrades enabled and only mentions disabling them as an exception case under 'When to Consider Manual Upgrades' section

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades are useful for specific scenarios (emergency patches, accelerating the schedule) but are the exception, not the rule
assertions.reason: While response mentions manual upgrades as an option and provides commands, it doesn't specifically explain scenarios like emergency patches or accelerating schedules - it's more generic about when manual control might be needed

assertions.expectation: Recommends enrolling in scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance) for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response mentions setting up logging for upgrade events but does not specifically mention scheduled upgrade notifications or the 72-hour advance notice feature

assertions.expectation: Suggests checking the GKE release schedule for longer-range planning (~1 month best-case for new minors)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention checking GKE release schedules or provide any guidance on longer-range planning timelines for new minor versions

assertions.expectation: Mentions release channel selection as the primary lever for upgrade cadence (Stable = slower, Regular = balanced)
assertions.reason: Response mentions that Regular channel gets updates ~2-3 months after release but doesn't explain release channels as the primary lever for controlling upgrade cadence or compare Stable vs Regular options
